commit.message:
feat: provide boundary support for agent modules (#780)

## Description
Enable any agent module to run its AI agent inside Coder's Agent
Boundaries.
The agentapi module handles boundary installation, config setup, and
wrapper
script creation, then exports AGENTAPI_BOUNDARY_PREFIX for consuming
modules
to use in their start scripts.

Supports three boundary installation modes:
- coder boundary subcommand (default, Coder v2.30+)
- Standalone binary via install script (use_boundary_directly)
- Compiled from source (compile_boundary_from_source)

Users must provide a boundary config.yaml with their allowlist and
settings when enabling boundary.

Closes #457

## Type of Change
- [x] Feature/enhancement

## Module Information
**Path:** `registry/coder/modules/agentapi`
**Breaking change:** No

## Testing & Validation
- [x] Tests pass (`bun test`)
- [x] Code formatted (`bun fmt`)
- [x] Changes tested locally

---------

Co-authored-by: Shane White <shane.white@cloudsecure.ltd>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: 35C4n0r <70096901+35C4n0r@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add background notification for new PR comments (#18)

* Initial plan

* Add notification when new comments are detected on pull requests

Track unresolved comment counts across refresh cycles and show a VS Code
information message when the count increases on any pull request. The
first fetch initializes the baseline without triggering notifications.

- Single PR: "New comment on PR #123: PR Title"
- Multiple PRs: "New comments on N pull requests"

Co-authored-by: alexanderwuytsIS <130978619+alexanderwuytsIS@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review: use 'New unresolved comments' phrasing for accuracy

Co-authored-by: alexanderwuytsIS <130978619+alexanderwuytsIS@users.noreply.github.com>

* Move comment notification polling to background timer

The periodic interval now calls pollForNewComments() which independently
fetches PR data and checks for new comments, regardless of whether the
sidebar panel is visible. Previously notifications only fired inside
getChildren() which VS Code skips when the tree view is not visible.

Also extracts shared fetchPullRequests() method to reduce duplication
between pollForNewComments() and getChildren().

Co-authored-by: alexanderwuytsIS <130978619+alexanderwuytsIS@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix notifications not showing: restore checkForNewComments in getChildren and add initial poll on activation

The previous refactor removed checkForNewComments from getChildren(),
so the baseline comment counts were only established on the first
polling interval (5 min). This meant notifications couldn't appear
until the second interval (10 min), making it seem like they were
broken.

Fix by:
1. Restoring checkForNewComments() in getChildren() for immediate
   baseline when sidebar is visible
2. Adding initial pollForNewComments() call on activation for
   immediate baseline when sidebar is not visible

Co-authored-by: alexanderwuytsIS <130978619+alexanderwuytsIS@users.noreply.github.com>

* Change default polling interval from 300s to 60s

Co-authored-by: alexanderwuytsIS <130978619+alexanderwuytsIS@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add changelog entry for background PR comment notifications

Co-authored-by: alexanderwuytsIS <130978619+alexanderwuytsIS@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: alexanderwuytsIS <130978619+alexanderwuytsIS@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: lucacelea <43340574+lucacelea@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add assigned work items picker to PR creation flow (#17)

* Initial plan

* Add assigned work items picker during PR creation

- Add WorkItem interface and getAssignedWorkItems() API function using WIQL
  query to fetch non-done work items assigned to current user, then batch-fetch
  their details (id, title, state, type) in groups of up to 200
- Add azureDevops.showAssignedWorkItems setting (boolean, default true) to
  package.json so users can opt out of the picker
- During createPullRequest(), after the draft selection, show a canPickMany
  QuickPick of assigned work items; pre-select any item whose ID was parsed from
  the current branch name; use the selected set for both PR linking and work
  item state transitions
- Cache workItemProject to avoid redundant getWorkItemProject() calls

Co-authored-by: alexanderwuytsIS <130978619+alexanderwuytsIS@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: remove redundant byte patterns in generator test inputs

In three test files, the embedded input source used alue is >= 0 and <= 1
with a  yte parameter. Since  yte is unsigned (range 0-255), the >= 0
subpattern is always true and the compiler emits CS9335 (redundant pattern).

AssertDiagnosticsCount applies no severity filter, so even this hidden
diagnostic caused the AssertDiagnosticsCount(0, newCompilationDiagnostics)
assertion to fail in three tests:
- ComparableValueTypeGenerationTests.GeneratesValueType
- AddableValueTypeGenerationTests.GeneratesValueType
- EquatableValueTypeGenerationTests.Generates_type_given_minimal_specification

Fix: replace alue is >= 0 and <= 1 with alue is <= 1 in all affected
test input strings (4 occurrences across 3 files).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: open-dotnet
repository.description: Libraries and tools supporting DSE Open systems and technologies.

commit.message:
feat: implement OpenViking-inspired capabilities (hotness score, used() feedback, L0/L1/L2, fast/quality paths, memory taxonomy, tool stats)

Borrowed 5 high-value capabilities from volcengine/OpenViking, maximally
leveraging openJiuwen framework built-ins (AgenticRetriever for quality path,
rrf_fusion framework already available).

New capabilities:
- Hotness Score: sigmoid(log1p(active_count)) × exp(-λ×age_days) blended
  into RRF fusion at alpha=0.2 (context_agent/core/memory/hotness.py)
- used() feedback API: POST /context/used increments active_count on
  confirmed context items, driving Hotness Score
- MemoryCategory 6-taxonomy: profile/preferences/entities/events/cases/patterns
  with AggregationRequest.category_filter support
- ContextLevel L0/L1/L2: ABSTRACT/OVERVIEW/DETAIL levels with
  AggregationRequest.max_level progressive expansion
- fast/quality dual-path: mode='quality' activates AgenticRetriever
  (openJiuwen-native) for complex tasks; mode='fast' (default) uses
  HybridRetriever for low-latency simple queries
- Tool Performance Memory: ToolContextGovernor.record_tool_result() +
  POST /tools/result endpoint; unreliable tools rank lower in select_tools()

Files changed:
- context_agent/models/context.py: +ContextLevel, +MemoryCategory enums,
  +ContextItem.{level,category,active_count,updated_at} fields
- context_agent/core/memory/hotness.py: new hotness pure function module
- context_agent/core/retrieval/search_coordinator.py: hotness-blended RRF
- context_agent/core/memory/working_memory.py: +mark_used() method
- context_agent/core/memory/tiered_router.py: +record_usage() method
- context_agent/core/retrieval/tool_governor.py: +record_tool_result(), +_sort_by_success_rate()
- context_agent/adapters/ltm_adapter.py: +agentic_search() with AgenticRetriever fallback
- context_agent/orchestration/context_aggregator.py: +mode/max_level/category_filter params
- context_agent/api/schemas.py: +ContextUsedRequest/Response, +ToolResultRequest/Response
- context_agent/api/http_handler.py: +POST /context/used, +POST /tools/result
- context_agent/api/router.py: +mark_used(), +record_tool_result() facade methods
- tests/unit/test_openviking_capabilities.py: 38 new unit tests (all passing)
- docs/architecture-design.md: +Appendix D with capability details
- docs/agent-integration-guide.md: +Section 9 with usage guide

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: permission revert on WS  dedup + retry unconfirmedreconnect

 WS drops before server
 reconnect replays from DB (no permission_response
 permission shows buttons again, requiring a second Allow.

Fixes:
1. Live permission_request dedup: skip if element with same requestId
   already exists in DOM (handles bridge restore re-emission)
2. Track unconfirmed responses: state.unconfirmedPermissions Map records
   responses sent but not yet confirmed by server (permission_resolved)
3. Retry on reconnect: retryUnconfirmedPermissions() resends pending
   responses after loadNewEvents, re-applying optimistic UI

7 new tests covering dedup, tracking, retry, skip-resolved, cleanup.
331 unit + 48 E2E pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: webagent

fix: add flex layout guards for iOS PWA safe-area support

Without flex-shrink: 0 on #header and #input-area, the flex
container could compress them when safe-area padding increases
the input area height. Without min-height: 0 on #messages, the
flex item cannot shrink below its content size, causing overflow.

These are standard CSS flex + safe-area best practices for iOS
PWA standalone mode, complementing the viewport-fit=cover and
env(safe-area-inset-bottom) padding already on #input-area.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: prevent message loss when WebSocket is disconnected

sendMessage() was adding user messages to the DOM optimistically
before calling ws.send(). If the WebSocket was closing or closed,
the send would silently fail. On reconnect, the incremental reload
removes DOM content after the sync boundary and replays from DB
but the message was never received by the server, so it vanishes.

Add wsReady() check (readyState === OPEN) before all three send
paths: regular prompts, bash commands, and image upload callbacks.
When disconnected, show a warning instead of sending. For regular
prompts the check runs before addMessage, avoiding the optimistic-
add-then-lose race entirely.

3 new unit tests for the disconnected send paths (324/324 pass).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: add safe-area-inset-bottom for iOS PWA input area

Without viewport-fit=cover, env(safe-area-inset-*) variables are not
available. In iOS PWA standalone mode with black-translucent status
bar, the home indicator overlaps the bottom of the page, cutting off
the input area.

Add viewport-fit=cover to the viewport meta tag and apply
safe-area-inset-bottom padding to #input-area so controls stay above
the home indicator.

commit.message:
fix: remove autofocus that causes iOS keyboard to not appear

iOS Safari requires focus to originate from a user gesture for the
virtual keyboard to appear. The HTML autofocus attribute focuses the
textarea on page load without a gesture, putting it into a "focused
but no keyboard" state where subsequent taps also fail to open the
keyboard.

This was the remaining trigger after c18e41c removed programmatic
.focus() calls from WebSocket event callbacks.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Remove push notification merge window

permission_request notifications, causing missed alerts when the

Add missing workflow files from OSPO actions

Added standard workflows used across OSPO GitHub Actions:

- auto-labeler.yml: Automatically labels PRs based on release-drafter config
- copilot-setup-steps.yml: Documents Copilot CLI setup for contributors
- linter.yaml: Runs super-linter on PRs to enforce code quality
- pr-title.yml: Validates PR titles follow conventional commit format
- scorecard.yml: Runs OpenSSF Scorecard security analysis
- stale.yaml: Closes stale issues after 35 days of inactivity
- release-drafter.yml: Configuration for auto-labeler and release notes

These workflows provide:
- Automated PR labeling for release notes
- Code quality enforcement via linting
- Security scanning via Scorecard
- Stale issue management
- PR title validation

Brings pr-conflict-detector in line with other OSPO actions like
issue-metrics, stale-repos, contributors, etc.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(grammar): replace ^ exponentiation with C bitwise operators (#229)

Remove the non-standard McCode exponentiation rule (^ was used for
pow() but conflicts with C's bitwise XOR semantics). Add all four
missing C bitwise operators in correct precedence order.

Grammar changes (McCommon.g4):
- Remove ExpressionExponentiation (^ as pow; use pow() instead)
- Add ExpressionBitwiseAnd  (&,  after shifts)
- Add ExpressionBitwiseXor  (^,  after &)
- Add ExpressionBitwiseOr   (|,  after ^)
- Add ExpressionBitwiseNot  (~,  with unary prefix ops)

Implementation:
- sympy_classes.py: CBitwiseAnd, CBitwiseOr, CBitwiseXor, CBitwiseNot
  added to SYMPY_NAMESPACE for serialization round-trip
- printer.py: _print_CBitwise* methods for McCodeCPrinter and
  McCodePyPrinter (both use identical C/Python syntax)
- visitor.py: visitExpressionBitwise{And,Or,Xor,Not} added;
  visitExpressionExponentiation removed
- expr.py: __and__, __rand__, __or__, __ror__, __xor__, __rxor__,
  __invert__ dunder methods wrapping CBitwise* classes

Tests:
- Parse round-trip tests for all four operators
- Dunder method tests (operator syntax on Expr objects)
- DataType.int assertion for all bitwise results

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

  (CStructAccess, CPointerAccess, CTernary, CArrayIndex, CIntDiv,
  CLeftShift, CRightShift, CRound, CFunctionCall, CInitializerList,
  CAnd, COr, CNot), UNSET_SYMPY sentinel, SYMPY_NAMESPACE dict
- Add printer.py: McCodeCPrinter (extends C99CodePrinter) + McCodePyPrinter
  with compact relational operators, custom C-construct handlers,
  and repr()-based float printing for decimal precision
- Add legacy_conv.py: exprnode_to_sympy() migration helper

Rewrite expr.py:
- Plain Python class with __slots__ (not msgspec.Struct)
- All factory classmethods (int, float, str, id, parameter, array, parse)
- Arithmetic/comparison operators; .eq/.ne/.lt/.gt/.le/.ge build Exprs
- Numeric __eq__ with absolute+relative tolerance + symbolic simplify fallback
- CFunctionCall.free_symbols excludes function name (depends_on fix)
- Scalar * vector element-wise distribution in __mul__

Update visitor.py, comp/visitor.py, instance.py, instr.py, orientation.py,
c_listener.py, io/utils.py with new Expr factory calls.

- orientation.py: _cos_sin_one_zero uses Expr.int(1)/Expr.int(0);
  cos_value/sin_value return Expr.int() for exact angles (0°, 90°, etc.)
- CIntDiv.eval constant-folds integer literals (7//3 → 2)
- CFunctionCall.free_symbols property excludes function-name arg

Update tests to use new API:
- Remove BinaryOp/UnaryOp/Value internal-structure checks
- test_expression.py: precedence test via str() comparison,
  instrument_parameter via str() check
- test_expression_extended.py: division-by-zero → zoo, array indexing
  via parse(), vector operations via Expr.array()
- test_component_vector_parameter.py: .expr → ._exprs, isinstance sympy.Symbol
- test_behavior_expr_orient.py: remove OpStyle import

Remove ExprNode hierarchy (nodes.py, legacy_conv.py); complete SymPy migration

- Delete nodes.py (861 lines) and legacy_conv.py (124 lines)
- Delete ValueIO, _op_io from hdf5.py; add ExprIO using to_dict/from_dict
- Remove Value/BinaryOp/UnaryOp/TrinaryOp exports from common/__init__.py
  and expression/__init__.py
- Fix cos_from_sin_value() in orientation.py to use SymPy/CFunctionCall
  instead of old UnaryOp internals
- Update test_expression.py and test_expression_extended.py to use new
  Expr API (no old Value/BinaryOp/etc. imports)
- Update test_empty_trace.py, test_instr.py, test_extract.py to use Expr
- Remove instr.py / instance.py imports of removed Value type
- Add sympy>=1.12 to pyproject.toml dependencies

Map known C math functions to native SymPy in visitor

Replace CFunctionCall wrapping for sin, cos, tan, asin, acos, atan,
atan2, exp, log, sqrt, cbrt, pow, abs, fabs, floor, ceil and fmod with
their native SymPy equivalents in visitExpressionFunctionCall.

Each entry in _MATH_FUNCS carries an explicit return DataType so that
future integer-returning functions (isnan, isinf, …) can be added
without being mis-typed as DataType.float.

Benefits:
- SymPy can now apply algebraic identities: sin²+cos²→1, sin(0)→0,
  sqrt(4)→2, sin(asin(x))→x, etc.
- C99CodePrinter already knows how to emit all these (fabs for Abs,
  ceil for ceiling, fmod for Mod, pow for Pow) so no printer changes
  were needed.
- cos_from_sin_value() in orientation.py updated to handle both native
  sympy.sin objects and legacy CFunctionCall('sin',…) wrappers.
- Two test assertions updated to reflect SymPy's canonical form:
  sin(−x)→−sin(x) and order-independent Add printing.

* Refactor Expr to msgspec.Struct for native serialization

- Change ObjectType, ShapeType, DataType from Enum to IntEnum so
  msgspec can encode/decode them natively as integers
- Replace Expr.__slots__-based class with Expr(msgspec.Struct, dict=True)
  with fields: exprs: list[_str], data_type, shape_type, object_type
- __post_init__ normalises the exprs field (accepts sympy.Basic, list of
  sympy.Basic, or list[str]) and auto-promotes scalar to vector
- _exprs is now a cached property backed by __dict__['_cache']
- Add explicit __ne__ to override msgspec._StructMixin.__ne__ so that
  != correctly delegates to our custom __eq__
- Use _str = str alias to avoid Expr.str classmethod shadowing the
  built-in str when msgspec evaluates type annotations
- Remove data_type/shape_type properties and setters; they are now
  direct struct fields (settable as before)
- Update all internal self._data_type/self._shape_type/self._object_type
  references to the public field names
- Update comp/visitor.py and tests to use public field names


* Fix Expr type hints: remove _str alias, rename .int/.str classmethods

- Remove the _str = str alias hack: with 'from __future__ import annotations'
  in effect, msgspec resolves 'list[str]' in module globals where str is the
  built-in, not Expr.str. Revert exprs field annotation to list[str].

- Rename Expr.int → Expr.integer and Expr.str → Expr.string to avoid
  shadowing the built-in int/str names, which caused IDEs to misresolve
  return-type annotations like '-> str' or '-> int' inside and around Expr.

- Add type: ignore comments on the __post_init__ lines that intentionally
  accept sympy.Basic at construction time (internal factory-method path);
  document this clearly so the public struct constructor remains correctly
  typed as (exprs: list[str], ...).

131 call sites updated (107 Expr.int → Expr.integer, 24 Expr.str → Expr.string).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Restore is_float and is_int convenience properties on Expr

The old Value class (deleted in nodes.py removal) had is_float and
is_int properties. Add equivalent shortcuts to Expr delegating to
data_type, restoring backward compatibility for downstream code that
calls expr.is_float or expr.is_int directly.


Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

# Downstream Verification
Minor changes are likely needed in `mccode-antlr` consumers. Thus far, I have successfully migrated `moreniius`, `restage`, `niess`, plus secondary dependent `mccode-plumber`, to make use of this branch. As such I think it likely that any code dependent on `mccode-antlr` can be adapted without loss of functionality.

repository.name: mccode-antlr
repository.description: McCode grammar implemented with ANTLR4

commit.message:
fix(ci): reduce API payload size (#14)

* fix(ci): reduce API payload size

Evaluate call (~44KB -> ~26KB):
- Remove README.md from codebase context (12KB of user docs adds
  no value for classification/planning)
- Halve the char cap from 60k to 20k chars (~5k tokens)

Implement call (~44KB -> ~3-8KB typically):
- Load only ev.plan.affected_files instead of the full codebase;
  the evaluation already identified which files need changes
- Falls back to all Lua files if affected_files is empty/unreadable
- Cap raised to 40k chars since this call needs richer context

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

the file types that it will emit, is either lua, markdown, txt (vim doc, in doc folder), others are no consideration for now

commit.message:
Fix login flow, tunnel status, admin rename, consolidate portal

- fix: login server action called wrong endpoint (GET→POST /api/portal/login)
- fix: isRedirectError re-throw to prevent catch block swallowing Next.js redirects
- fix: tunnel status stuck offline — add UpdateTunnelOnline() called on agent poll
- fix: NEXT_PUBLIC_CONTROL_API_URL use relative /control path to avoid mixed content
- feat: admin can inline-rename tunnel hostnames (pencil icon in tunnels drawer)
- feat: new API route /api/admin/routes/[id] for hostname rename with uniqueness check
- refactor: /portal login page replaced with redirect to /login (single entry point)
- fix: /portal/dashboard redirects to /login (not /portal), handles tunnel_id query param
  from server action redirect to initialize localStorage credentials

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
